topic.name: Historical Architecture and Sites
topic.description: The documents discuss significant historical architectural sites and cities across different cultures and regions.

Document 1: Calakmul is a Maya archaeological site in the Mexican state of Campeche.
Calakmul was one of the largest and most powerful ancient cities ever uncovered in the Maya lowlands.
Document 2: Marrakesh is a major city of the Kingdom of Morocco.
The red walls of the city have given it the nickname of the 'Red City.'
Document 3: The medieval cathedrals of England constitute a major aspect of the country’s artistic heritage.
These buildings serve as central churches for administrative regions.
Document 4: The Central Troy Historic District is described as one of the most perfectly preserved 19th-century downtowns.
It has nearly 700 properties in a variety of architectural styles.
Document 5: Antananarivo is the capital and largest city in Madagascar.
The city is the political, economic, educational and cultural heart of Madagascar.
Document 6: Omaha is the largest city in the state of Nebraska.
Omaha's pioneer period began in 1854.
Document 7: The architecture of Scotland includes all human building within the modern borders of Scotland.
The earliest surviving houses go back around 9500 years.
Document 8: The Grand Palace is a complex of buildings at the heart of Bangkok, Thailand.
The palace has been the official residence of the Kings of Siam since 1782.
Document 9: Ludlow Castle is a ruined medieval fortification in the town of the same name in Shropshire.
The castle was probably founded by Walter de Lacy after the Norman conquest.
Document 10: Castles have played an important military, economic and social role in Great Britain and Ireland.
The Normans began to build motte and bailey and ringworks castles in large numbers.

topic.name: Mushroom Species Identification
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of mushrooms, their characteristics, habitats, and taxonomy.

Document 1: Lactarius fallax, commonly known as the velvety milk cap, is a species of fungus in the Russulaceae family.
It is a fairly common species in the Pacific Northwest region of North America.
Document 2: Mycena galericulata is a mushroom species commonly known as the common bonnet.
The mushrooms have caps with distinct radial grooves, particularly at the margin.
Document 3: Amanita gemmata, commonly known as the gemmed Amanita or the jonquil Amanita, is an agaric mushroom of the family Amanitaceae.
It is a toxic mushroom that has muscarine.
Document 4: Mycena fuscoaurantiaca is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
The mushroom has a brownish-orange conical cap.
Document 5: Mycena nidificata is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family of the Agaricales.
The dark brown irregularly wrinkled cap measures up to 25 mm in diameter.
Document 6: Boletus rubroflammeus is a species of bolete fungus in the family Boletaceae.
The fruit bodies of the fungus have caps that are deep red to purplish red.
Document 7: Mycena maculata, commonly known as the reddish-spotted Mycena, is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family.
The saprobic fungus is found in Europe and North America.
Document 8: Pluteus nevadensis is a species of fungus in the agaric family Pluteaceae.
The species was described by Olivia Rodríguez in 2010.
Document 9: Pulveroboletus bembae is a species of fungus in the Boletaceae family that was first described in 2009.
The fungus grows in a mycorrhizal relationship with Gilbertiodendron dewevrei.
Document 10: Lactifluus deceptivus, commonly known as the deceiving milkcap, is a common species of fungus in the Russulaceae family.
The fruit bodies are edible, but have a bitter taste that can be removed with cooking.

topic.name: Popular Music Releases
topic.description: This cluster discusses various popular music releases across different genres and artists, highlighting their background, reception, and commercial success.

Document 1: Bachata # 1 's , Vol . 3 is a compilation album released by Machete Music on March 30 , 2010.
The album includes tracks recorded by several artists from the bachata genre.
Document 2: Let Yourself Go is a song by American punk rock band Green Day.
The song received positive critical reception and charted at # 2 at UK Rock Chart.
Document 3: Hot Tottie is a song by recording artist Usher.
It received positive reviews, with many critics noting it as a standout from the EP.
Document 4: Paparazzi is a song recorded in the Japanese language by South Korean girl group Girls ' Generation.
The single was a commercial success in Japan, peaking atop the Japan Hot 100.
Document 5: Gate is a song recorded by Japanese group Kisida Kyoudan & The Akebosi Rockets.
It received positive reviews from music critics.
Document 6: Lollipop is the debut song recorded by Romanian recording artist Alexandra Stan for her debut studio album, Saxobeats.
Music critics praised it for being a catchy pop song.
Document 7: Give It Up to Me is a song by Colombian singer-songwriter Shakira featuring American rapper Lil Wayne.
The song received generally favorable reviews from music critics.
Document 8: Above and Beyoncé is a remix and video album by American R & B singer Beyoncé.
The remix version of 'Ego' included on the album was nominated for Best Rap / Sung Collaboration at the 52nd Grammy Awards.
Document 9: Here with Me is a song by Christian rock band, MercyMe.
It attained positive critical reception and peaked at No. 1 on multiple Christian radio chart formats.

topic.description: The documents discuss various military aircraft, their design, roles, and historical significance in air combat and support operations.

topic.name: Cricket and Baseball Athletes
topic.description: The documents discuss notable athletes in cricket and baseball, highlighting their careers, achievements, and contributions to their respective sports.

Document 1: Pat Burrell is an American former professional baseball outfielder who played in Major League Baseball.
He played with the Philadelphia Phillies, the Tampa Bay Rays, and the San Francisco Giants.
Document 2: Claire Taylor is a former cricketer who represented England more than 150 times between 1998 and 2011.
Taylor was the first woman to be named a Wisden Cricketer of the Year.
Document 3: Keith Miller was an Australian test cricketer and a Royal Australian Air Force pilot during World War II.
Miller is widely regarded as Australia 's greatest ever all-rounder.
Document 5: Dinesh Karthik is an Indian wicketkeeper-batsman who made his debut for the Indian cricket team in 2004.
Karthik was a batsman in his junior career, but turned to wicket-keeping in order to improve his future prospects.
Document 6: Virat Kohli is an Indian international cricketer and is often rated as one of the best batsmen in the world.
He captains the Indian team in Test cricket and is its vice-captain in limited overs formats.
Document 4: The Australian cricket team in England in 1948 was captained by Don Bradman, who was making his fourth and final tour of England.
The team is famous for being the first Test match side to play an entire tour of England without losing a match.

topic.name: State Highways and Interstates
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways and interstate routes in the United States, detailing their lengths, connections, historical development, and geographical significance.

Document 1: Maryland Route 32 is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The state highway also intersects the four primary highways connecting Baltimore and Washington.
Document 2: Tonkin Highway is a 44-kilometre-long north-south highway in Perth, Western Australia.
Planning for the route began in the 1950s.
Document 3: Interstate 695 is a 51.46-mile-long full beltway Interstate Highway extending around Baltimore, Maryland.
The route is an auxiliary route of I-95.
Document 4: Mitchell Freeway is a 30-kilometre-long freeway in the northern suburbs of Perth, Western Australia.
Planning for the route began in the 1950s.
Document 5: Interstate 95 is an Interstate highway running along the East Coast of the United States.
I-95 is the only primary (2-digit) Interstate highway that enters Delaware.
Document 6: U.S. Route 15 is a part of the U.S. Highway System that runs from Walterboro, South Carolina to Painted Post, New York.
The highway connects the county seat of Frederick with Point of Rocks, Leesburg, Virginia.
Document 7: Interstate 70 is a part of the Interstate Highway System that runs from Cove Fort, Utah to Baltimore, Maryland.
The Interstate Highway connects Baltimore — and Washington via I-270 — with Western Maryland.
Document 8: Maryland Route 2 is the longest state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The route runs concurrent with MD 4 through much of Calvert County.
Document 9: U.S. Route 13 is a U.S. highway running from Fayetteville, North Carolina north to Morrisville, Pennsylvania.
US 13 connects many important cities and towns in Delaware.
Document 10: Delaware Route 1 is a 103.02-mile-long state highway in the U.S. state of Delaware.
The route runs from the Maryland border in Fenwick Island, Sussex County.

topic.name: Political Figures and Elections
topic.description: This cluster discusses various political figures, their roles, and significant elections in the United States and other countries.

1: Gary Johnson is an American businessman, politician and the Libertarian Party nominee for President of the United States in the 2016 election.
Johnson announced his candidacy for president on April 21, 2011, as a Republican, on a libertarian platform emphasizing the United States public debt.
2: Parliamentary elections were held in Finland on 17 April 2011 after the termination of the previous parliamentary term.
The election resulted in a breakthrough for the populist True Finns.
3: Steve Beshear is an American attorney and Democratic Party politician who was the 61st Governor of Kentucky from 2007 to 2015.
He served in the Kentucky House of Representatives from 1974 to 1979.
4: Stephen Harper is a Canadian politician and member of Parliament who served as the 22nd Prime Minister of Canada.
He was the first prime minister to come from the modern Conservative Party of Canada.
5: Al Gore is an American politician and environmentalist who served as the 45th Vice President of the United States from 1993 to 2001.
He was the Democratic Party's nominee for President in 2000.
6: Jennifer Brunner is an American politician of the Democratic Party who served as the Ohio Secretary of State; Brunner was the first woman to serve in this capacity.
As Secretary of State, Brunner was actively involved in evaluating and adjusting statewide election systems.
7: Singapore has a multi-party parliamentary system of representative democracy in which the President of Singapore is the head of state.
Parliamentary elections in Singapore are required to be held regularly to elect the Parliament through universal suffrage.
8: Ted Kennedy was a United States Senator from Massachusetts and a member of the Democratic Party.
He was the second most senior member of the Senate when he died.
9: George W. Bush is an American politician who served as the 43rd President of the United States from 2001 to 2009.
He was elected president in 2000 after a close and controversial election against Al Gore.
10: Bill Clinton is an American politician who was the 42nd President of the United States from 1993 to 2001.
Clinton was previously Governor of Arkansas from 1979 to 1981 and 1983 to 1992.

topic.name: Royal Navy Warships
topic.description: This cluster discusses various classes of warships built for the Royal Navy and their service history during significant naval conflicts.

Document 1: Warrior-class ironclads were a class of two warships built for the Royal Navy between 1859 and 1862
The ships spent their first commission with the Channel Fleet before being rearmed with new rifled muzzle-loading guns in the late 1860s.
Document 2: HMS Brilliant was a B-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy around 1930
During World War II, Brilliant spent most of the war on convoy escort duties in the English Channel.
Document 3: HMS Boadicea was a B-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy around 1930
During World War II, the ship spent the bulk of the war on convoy escort duty in British waters.
Document 6: The Minotaur class was a three-ship class of armoured cruisers built in the first decade of the twentieth century for the Royal Navy.
They were significantly larger and more heavily armed than their predecessors.
Document 9: HMS Triumph was the second of the two Swiftsure-class pre-dreadnought battleships of the Royal Navy.
She participated in the hunt for the German East Asia Squadron of Maximilian Graf von Spee.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: A historical overview of the annual rowing competition between the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.

Document 1: The Boat Race is an annual side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course.
Document 2: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
First held in 1829, the race takes place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course.
Document 3: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge along the River Thames.
The rivalry is a major point of honour between the two universities.

The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course.

The rivalry is a major point of honour between the two universities.

The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course.
Document 7: The Boat Race is an annual rowing eight competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge.
First held in 1829, the competition is a 4.2-mile (6.8 km) race along The Championship Course on the River Thames.
Document 8: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
The rivalry is a major point of honour between the two universities.
Document 9: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between the boat clubs of University of Oxford and University of Cambridge along the River Thames.
The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course.
Document 10: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
First held in 1829, the race takes place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course.

topic.name: State Highways in the U.S.
topic.description: This cluster discusses various state highways across different U.S. states, including their routes, historical designations, and geographical contexts.

Document 1: Delaware Route 3 is a state highway northeast of Wilmington in New Castle County, Delaware.
The route was built as a state highway during the 1920s and 1930s.
Document 2: State Road 930 is an Indiana State Road that runs between Fort Wayne and New Haven in the US state of Indiana.
SR 930 was designated in 1998, and replaced segments of US 30 in Fort Wayne and New Haven.
Document 3: State Route 513 is a 3.35-mile-long state highway in the U.S. state of Washington.
SR 513 was created during the 1964 state highway renumbering.
Document 4: Delaware Route 18 is a state highway located in Sussex County, Delaware.
DE 18 runs concurrent with DE 404 from its intersection with that highway.
Document 5: Delaware Route 42 is a state highway in Kent County, Delaware.
The road was built as a state highway during the 1920s and 1930s.
Document 6: Delaware Route 6 is a state highway in Kent County, Delaware.
The route passes through rural areas of northern Kent County.
Document 7: Delaware Route 16 is an east-west state highway in Delaware, mainly across northern Sussex County.
DE 16 was built as a state highway during the 1920s and 1930s.
Document 8: Delaware Route 300 is an 11.83-mile-long state highway in Kent County, Delaware.
The route was first built as a state highway in the 1920s and 1930s.
Document 9: Delaware Route 26 is a state highway in Sussex County, Delaware.
The route passes through rural areas of southeastern Sussex County.
Document 10: State Route 4 is a 62.27-mile-long state highway, serving the southwestern region of the U.S. state of Washington.
The highway is designated as part of the Lewis and Clark Trail Scenic Byway.

topic.name: Hollywood Actors and Filmmakers
topic.description: This cluster discusses prominent actors and filmmakers in Hollywood, their careers, achievements, and contributions to the film industry.

Document 1: Meryl Streep is particularly known for her versatility in her roles, transformation into the characters she plays, and her accent adaptation.
Nominated for 19 Academy Awards in total, Streep has more nominations than any other actor or actress in history.
Document 2: Spencer Tracy was noted for his natural style and versatility.
Tracy was nominated for nine Academy Awards for Best Actor and won two.
Document 3: Cary Grant became known for his transatlantic accent, light-hearted approach to acting and comic timing.
He established a name for himself in vaudeville in the 1920s and toured the United States before moving to Hollywood.
Document 4: Clint Eastwood rose to international fame with his role as the Man with No Name in Sergio Leone's Dollars trilogy.
Eastwood won Academy Awards for Best Director and Best Picture for his work in Unforgiven and Million Dollar Baby.
Document 6: Vikram is an Indian film actor who predominantly appears in Tamil language films and has won seven Filmfare Awards.
He made his debut in the 1990 film En Kadhal Kanmani.
Document 10: R. Madhavan has received two Filmfare Awards and is described as one of the few actors in India who is able to achieve pan-Indian appeal.
Madhavan began his acting career with television guest appearances.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling History
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss the history and notable figures in professional wrestling, focusing on their careers, championships, and significant events.

1: Dolph Ziggler, best known by the ring name Dolph Ziggler, is an American professional wrestler, stand-up comedian, and actor.
Nemeth signed a development contract with WWE in 2004.
2: Bound for Glory IV was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by the Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) promotion.
The show was promoted as TNA's premier PPV event and their equivalent to the rival World Wrestling Entertainment's (WWE) WrestleMania.
3: Randal Keith 'Randy' Orton is an American professional wrestler and actor who is signed with WWE.
He is a 12-time world champion, having held the WWE Championship / WWE World Heavyweight Championship eight times.
4: WrestleMania XXIV was the twenty-fourth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
The event took place on March 30, 2008, at the Florida Citrus Bowl in Orlando, Florida.
5: Jeff Hardy is a professional wrestler, singer-songwriter, painter and musician currently signed to Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA).
As a singles wrestler, Hardy is a six-time world champion.
6: Paul Michael Levesque, better known by his ring name Triple H, is an American professional wrestler, corporate executive, and actor.
He is the Executive Vice President of Talent, Live Events, & Creative of WWE.
7: Adam Joseph Copeland, better known by his ring name Edge, is a Canadian actor and retired professional wrestler.
He is best known for his time with the American professional wrestling promotion WWE.
8: Michael Shawn Hickenbottom, better known by his ring name Shawn Michaels, is an American professional wrestling personality, television presenter and retired professional wrestler.
He was the co-founder and original leader of the successful stable, D-Generation X.
9: Matthew Moore 'Matt' Hardy is an American professional wrestler and wrestling promoter currently signed with Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA).
As a tag team wrestler, Hardy is a nine-time world tag team champion.
10: Kurt Steven Angle is an American professional wrestler, actor, former mixed martial artist and retired amateur wrestler.
In 1999, he signed a multi-year contract with then World Wrestling Federation (WWF).

topic.name: Ecology and Animal Behavior
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various aspects of ecology, animal behavior, and biological interactions among different species.

1: Equine nutrition is the feeding of horses, ponies, mules, donkeys, and other equines.
Correct and balanced nutrition is a critical component of proper horse care.
2: In evolutionary biology, mimicry is a similarity of one species to another that protects one or both.
Mimics occur in the same areas as their models.
3: Spiders are air-breathing arthropods that have eight legs and chelicerae with fangs that inject venom.
They are the largest order of arachnids and rank seventh in total species diversity among all other orders of organisms.
4: Myrmecia is a genus of ants first established by Danish zoologist Johan Christian Fabricius in 1804.
These ants are commonly known as 'bulldog ants' or 'jack jumper' ants, and are also associated with many other common names.
9: Ecology is the scientific analysis and study of interactions among organisms and their environment.
Topics of interest to ecologists include the diversity, distribution, amount (biomass), and number (population) of particular organisms.

topic.name: Linguistics and Philosophy
topic.description: The documents explore the intersections of language, philosophy, and religious thought, highlighting how linguistic differences and philosophical arguments shape human understanding and beliefs.

topic.name: Video Game Franchises
topic.description: The documents discuss various popular video game franchises and their development, gameplay, and cultural impact.

1: .hack is a series of single-player hack and slash developed for the PlayStation 2 console by CyberConnect2 and published by Bandai.
The games are part of a multimedia franchise called Project .hack which explores the mysterious origins of The World.
2: Resident Evil 4 is a survival horror third-person shooter video game developed and published by Capcom.
The sixth main entry in the Resident Evil horror series.
3: Mortal Kombat II is a competitive fighting game originally produced by Midway Games for the arcades in 1993.
It was the second game in the Mortal Kombat series, improving the gameplay and expanding the mythos of the original Mortal Kombat.
4: The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess is an action-adventure game developed and published by Nintendo for the GameCube and Wii home video game consoles.
It is the thirteenth installment in the The Legend of Zelda series.
5: Another World is a 1991 cinematic platformer action-adventure game designed by Éric Chahi for Delphine Software.
The game tells a story of Lester, a young scientist who, as a result of an experiment gone wrong, finds himself in a dangerous alien world.
6: Super Mario Bros. is a platform video game developed and published by Nintendo as a pseudo-sequel to the 1983 game, Mario Bros.
It is the first of the Super Mario series of games.
7: Wonder Boy in Monster Land is an action role-playing platform video game developed by Westone Bit Entertainment and released by Sega for arcade cabinets in 1987.
The game is the sequel to the 1986 game Wonder Boy.
8: The seventh generation includes consoles released since late 2005 by Nintendo, Microsoft, and Sony Computer Entertainment.
Video game consoles had become an important part of the global IT infrastructure.
9: Dragon Quest is the first role-playing video game (RPG) in the Dragon Quest media franchise.
It was developed by Chunsoft for the Family Computer (or Famicom for short) and published by Enix in Japan in 1986.
10: Dragon Quest is a series of internationally best-selling console role-playing video game (RPG) titles created by Yuji Horii.
The series has had a significant impact on the development of console RPGs.

topic.name: Tropical Storms and Hurricanes
topic.description: This cluster discusses various tropical storms and hurricanes, detailing their formation, impact, and meteorological history.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Danielle caused considerable flooding in the state of Texas during September 1980.
The storm also spawned several tornadoes in Texas.
Document 2: Tropical Storm Norman was a weak tropical cyclone that brought heavy rainfall to southwestern Mexico in October 2006.
One person was injured, and initially there were reports of two people missing due to the storm.
Document 3: Tropical Storm Julio in 2002 was a weak and short-lived tropical storm that made landfall along the southern Mexican coast.
Three fatalities and 18 injuries were reported from Julio when a bus flipped over.
Document 4: Hurricane Isaac was the final tropical cyclone of the 2006 Atlantic hurricane season.
Effects from the storm were minimal and limited to gusty winds, rough surf, and sporadic rainfall.
Document 5: Hurricane Jimena was the tenth named storm and second hurricane of the 2003 Pacific hurricane season.
However, the storm weakened as it passed south of the islands and the effects from Jimena were minimal.
Document 6: Hurricane Dean was the fourth named storm and second hurricane of the 1989 Atlantic hurricane season.
The storm left $8.9 million (1989 USD) and sixteen injuries across Bermuda.
Document 7: Tropical Storm Beryl made landfall just south of the United States – Mexico border in mid-August 2000, causing minimal damage.
One death was reported in Mexico due to drowning.
Document 8: Tropical Storm Becky was the second tropical cyclone and named storm of the 1970 Atlantic hurricane season.
Overall, damage was related to heavy rainfall that caused flooding in Tallahassee, Florida.
Document 9: Tropical Storm Alma of the 2008 Pacific hurricane season was the easternmost forming Pacific tropical cyclone on record.
In Costa Rica, heavy rainfall caused flooding and landslides, killing two and causing $35 million (USD) in damage.
Document 10: Hurricane Caroline was one of two tropical cyclones to affect northern Mexico during the 1975 Atlantic hurricane season.
After moving inland, Caroline quickly weakened and dissipated over the mountainous terrain of northeastern Mexico.

topic.name: American Revolutionary War Campaigns
topic.description: The documents discuss various military campaigns and battles during the American Revolutionary War, highlighting strategic maneuvers, key figures, and significant outcomes.

1: The Saratoga Campaign in 1777 was an attempt by the British high command for North America to gain military control of the strategically important Hudson River valley during the American Revolutionary War.
It ended in the surrender of the British army, which historian Edmund Morgan argues, 'was a great turning point of the war.'
2: The Yorktown or Virginia campaign was a series of military maneuvers and battles during the American Revolutionary War that culminated in the decisive Siege of Yorktown in October 1781.
The result of the campaign was the surrender of the British Army force of General Charles, Earl Cornwallis, an event that led directly to the beginning of serious peace negotiations.
4: The Battle of Long Island was the first major battle of the American Revolutionary War to take place after the United States declared its independence.
It was a victory for the British Army and the beginning of a successful campaign that gave them control of the strategically important city of New York.
7: The Battles of Lexington and Concord were the first military engagements of the American Revolutionary War.
The battles marked the outbreak of open armed conflict between the Kingdom of Great Britain and thirteen of its colonies on the mainland of British America.

topic.name: Alabama and Michigan Football Teams
topic.description: The documents discuss the performance and history of the Alabama Crimson Tide and Michigan Wolverines football teams during various NCAA seasons.

1: The 2009 Alabama Crimson Tide football team represented the University of Alabama in the 2009 NCAA Division I FBS football season.
They finished the season undefeated with a record of 14 – 0 and as national champions.
2: The 2013 Alabama Crimson Tide football team represented the University of Alabama in the 2013 NCAA Division I FBS football season.
They finished the season with a record of eleven wins and two losses and with a loss in the 2014 Sugar Bowl to Oklahoma.
4: The 1997 Michigan Wolverines football team represented the University of Michigan in the 1997 NCAA Division I-A football season.
Michigan compiled a perfect 12 – 0 record, won the Big Ten Conference championship, and was declared the national champion.
5: The 2010 Michigan Wolverines football team represented the University of Michigan in the 2010 NCAA Division I FBS football season.
Michigan became bowl eligible for the first time since 2007.

topic.name: Pacific Typhoon Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various Pacific typhoon seasons, detailing the formation, intensity, and impacts of tropical cyclones within those years.

Document 1: A total of 32 tropical depressions formed in 1986 in the Western Pacific over an eleven-month time span.
Most of the deaths attributed to typhons in 1986 were caused by Peggy and Wayne.
Document 2: A total of 30 tropical depressions formed in 1984 in the Western Pacific, of which 27 became tropical storms.
Eight tropical cyclones moved into mainland China, four struck Vietnam, four moved through the Philippines, and one cyclone moved into South Korea.
Document 3: A total of 35 tropical depressions formed this year in the Western Pacific, of which 29 became tropical storms.
Seven tropical cyclones moved through the Philippines this season.
Document 6: The 1985 Pacific hurricane season is the third most active Pacific hurricane season on record.
Despite the activity, only one system made landfall in 1985.
Document 10: The season had no official bounds and it ran year-round in 1994, but most tropical cyclones tend to form in the northwestern Pacific Ocean between May and November.
During the season, 25 systems either threatened land or made landfall, killing over 2,400 people.

topic.name: Medieval Hungarian Monarchs
topic.description: This topic encompasses the lives and reigns of various kings of Hungary during the medieval period, highlighting their familial ties, political struggles, and significant events.

Document 1: Constance of Antioch was the ruling Princess of Antioch from 1130 to 1163.
Constance was given in marriage to Raymond of Poitiers in 1136.
Document 2: Peter was King of Hungary twice.
His favoritism towards his foreign courtiers caused an uprising which ended with his 1041 deposition.
Document 3: Andrew III was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1290 and 1301.
He was the last male member of the House of Árpád.
Document 4: Géza I was King of Hungary from 1074 until his death.
He was the eldest son of King Béla I.
Document 5: Boniface of Savoy was a medieval Bishop of Belley and Archbishop of Canterbury.
He was the son of Thomas, Count of Savoy.
Document 6: Reginald Fitz Jocelin was a medieval Bishop of Bath and an Archbishop of Canterbury-elect in England.
He was a household clerk for Thomas Becket.
Document 7: Géza II was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1141 to 1162.
He was the oldest son of Béla the Blind.
Document 8: Andrew II was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1205 and 1235.
He ruled the Principality of Halych from 1188 until 1189.
Document 9: Stephen V was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1270 and 1272.
He was the oldest son of King Béla IV.
Document 10: Andrew I was King of Hungary from 1046 to 1060.
He strengthened the position of Christianity in the Kingdom of Hungary.

topic.name: Influential Rock Albums
topic.description: This topic encompasses landmark rock albums that have significantly shaped music history and culture, featuring innovative styles and themes from prominent artists.

1: Astral Weeks is the second studio album by Northern Irish singer-songwriter Van Morrison.
It has since been viewed as one of rock music's greatest and most important records.
2: Band of Gypsys is a live album by Jimi Hendrix and the first without his original group.
It contains previously unreleased songs and was the last full-length Hendrix album released before his death.
3: Abbey Road is the eleventh studio album by English rock band the Beatles.
It is a rock album that incorporates genres such as blues, pop and progressive rock.
4: Wonderwall Music is the soundtrack album to the 1968 film Wonderwall.
It was the first album to be issued on the Beatles' Apple record label.
10: All Things Must Pass is a triple album by English musician George Harrison.
The album reflects the influence of his musical activities with artists such as Bob Dylan, the Band, Delaney & Bonnie and Billy Preston.

topic.name: Fictional Characters in TV Shows
topic.description: This topic encompasses the development and significance of fictional characters across various television series, highlighting their roles, relationships, and story arcs.

1: Chris Pappas is a fictional character from the Australian soap opera Neighbours, played by James Mason.
Chris was the first prominent, ongoing male homosexual character in the show’s twenty-five-year history.
2: The fifth series of British science fiction television programme Doctor Who began on 3 April 2010 with 'The Eleventh Hour'.
This is the first series to feature Matt Smith as the eleventh incarnation of the Doctor.
3: Rose Tyler is a fictional character portrayed by Billie Piper in the British science fiction television series Doctor Who.
Rose is introduced as a new travelling companion of series protagonist, the Doctor.
4: Darren Osborne is a fictional character from the British Channel 4 soap opera Hollyoaks.
Darren was initially characterised as a 'bad boy' who has matured throughout his tenure.
5: Castiel is a fictional character portrayed by Misha Collins on the CW Television Network's American television series Supernatural.
Castiel develops friendships with both men and possesses a number of supernatural abilities.
6: The third season of the American television medical drama Grey's Anatomy commenced airing on the American Broadcasting Company (ABC) on September 21, 2006.
The season followed the continuation of the surgical residency of five young interns.
7: Grey's Anatomy is an American medical drama television series that premiered on ABC as a mid-season replacement on March 27, 2005.
The series focuses on the fictional lives of surgical interns, residents, and attendings.
8: The X-Files is an American science fiction horror drama television series created by Chris Carter.
The series revolves around FBI special agents Fox Mulder and Dana Scully who investigate X-Files.
9: Mercedes McQueen is a fictional character from the British Channel 4 soap opera Hollyoaks, played by Jennifer Metcalfe.
Mercedes is part of the McQueen family and is the longest serving McQueen on the series.
10: Susan Kennedy is a fictional character from the Australian soap opera Neighbours, played by Jackie Woodburne.
Susan became the longest-running female character in the show’s history.
